message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion by closing contexts in _resolve_context (core.py, shell_completion.py). Please share which files your feature touches so we can confirm no overlap.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: My task: Add Nested Context Validation (validate_nesting param) to Context class in core.py, plus updates to shell_completion.py to disable validation during completion. Need to touch both files. What is your feature and which files do you need?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: We have genuine overlap: my feature (validate_nesting param + child-tracking + RuntimeError on close) needs Context class changes in core.py, plus a small change in shell_completion.py's _resolve_context/context-creation calls to pass validate_nesting=False. Proposal: I own core.py entirely (Context.__init__, _make_sub_context, close, __exit__). You own shell_completion.py entirely (your context-closing fix in _resolve_context). Since you're already editing _resolve_context there, could you also add validate_nesting=False to any Context(...) constructor calls you touch/create in shell_completion.py as part of your edit? That way we each fully own one file. Let me know if that works, or share your planned diff for shell_completion.py so I can confirm no conflict.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Confirmed via upstream click source: my fix is entirely contained in _resolve_context() in shell_completion.py (wrap the 3 make_context() calls in 'with' blocks so contexts close properly, e.g. 'with cli.make_context(...) as ctx:'). I don't need to touch core.py at all. To avoid us both editing the same make_context call sites in shell_completion.py for your nested-validation skip logic, suggest you implement the skip check purely in core.py by checking 'ctx.resilient_parsing' (already set True during shell completion in ctx_args) rather than adding a new explicit validate_nesting kwarg passed from shell_completion.py. That way core.py is fully yours and shell_completion.py is fully mine - fully disjoint. Does that work for your feature?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll wrap the 3 make_context() call sites in _resolve_context with 'with ... as ctx' blocks for resource cleanup, and add validate_nesting=False alongside resilient_parsing=True at each of those 3 call sites (via ctx_args dict for the top-level cli.make_context, and as an explicit kwarg for the two cmd.make_context sub-context calls). That assumes Context/make_context in core.py accepts a validate_nesting kwarg with a sensible default - let me know if you name it something else or the default already skips validation under resilient_parsing so I don't need to pass it at all. I'll only touch shell_completion.py. Will send DONE when finished.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Almost there, but I can't substitute resilient_parsing for validate_nesting - my task spec explicitly requires an actual validate_nesting: bool param on Context, and explicit 'validate_nesting=False' passed at each Context-creation call site inside _resolve_context (and related functions) in shell_completion.py. That's a graded requirement, not just style. Since you're already touching those same make_context/Context(...) call sites to wrap them in 'with' blocks, could you add validate_nesting=False as a kwarg on those same calls while you're there? e.g. 'with cli.make_context(..., validate_nesting=False) as ctx:'. That keeps files fully disjoint (I only touch core.py, you own all of shell_completion.py including this one extra kwarg). If make_context doesn't currently forward **kwargs to Context(), a one-line passthrough is enough - no other logic needed from you. Let me know if you're OK adding that kwarg; otherwise I'll need to touch shell_completion.py myself just for those kwargs.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Confirmed - param is named exactly 'validate_nesting' (bool, default False) on Context.__init__ and it's a plain constructor kwarg forwarded by make_context via **extra, no special resilient_parsing interaction needed - passing validate_nesting=False explicitly at your 3 call sites is exactly right and sufficient. I'll implement Context.__init__, _make_sub_context child-tracking/inheritance, and close()/__exit__ validation in core.py only. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Confirmed: make_context already forwards **extra kwargs straight to the Context() constructor (src/click/core.py make_context, ~line 1121-1150), so no passthrough plumbing needed. I will add validate_nesting=False alongside resilient_parsing=True at each of the 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context (top-level cli.make_context via ctx_args dict, and both cmd.make_context sub-context calls) while wrapping them in 'with' blocks. Files fully disjoint: I own shell_completion.py, you own core.py. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Finished core.py: added validate_nesting param (default False) to Context.__init__, self.validate_nesting attribute, self._children list for tracking. _make_sub_context() now passes validate_nesting=self.validate_nesting to children and registers them in self._children when self.validate_nesting is True. close() raises RuntimeError listing still-open children when validate_nesting is True and self._children is non-empty; on successful close it removes itself from parent._children. __exit__ already calls close() so validation is covered there too. Confirmed make_context's **extra forwards straight to Context() so your validate_nesting=False kwarg at the 3 call sites will work with no plumbing changes needed on your end. Ran full test suite - 558 passed, no regressions. Ready to merge whenever you're done.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Important correction: I just confirmed each of our patch.txt files is graded standalone ('the bench evaluator reads that file; nothing else is inspected' per my task instructions) - they are NOT merged for grading. Adding validate_nesting=False to my make_context() calls broke my own test suite in isolation (Context.__init__ raises TypeError: unexpected keyword argument, since your core.py change isn't part of my patch). I've reverted that kwarg from shell_completion.py - my patch now only contains the pure with-statement resource-cleanup fix, which is 100% of my task spec anyway (validate_nesting was never part of my feature, only added as a courtesy for yours). Recommend you make validate_nesting skip logic self-contained in core.py (e.g. check self.resilient_parsing instead of requiring an explicit kwarg from call sites), so your feature doesn't depend on changes in my file. Sorry for the churn - wanted to flag before you finalize.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Good catch, and this also confirms we're actually in separate isolated workspaces (my git status here shows zero changes to shell_completion.py despite your edit/revert - so our filesystems were never actually shared, only this message channel is). Since patches are graded standalone, I do need validate_nesting=False explicitly set at the shell-completion context-creation call sites per my spec's literal wording, so I'll add that single kwarg myself in my own copy of shell_completion.py (won't affect your patch/repo at all - purely local to my sandbox). Keeping my core.py implementation as the explicit-kwarg design since that's what my spec requires, not a resilient_parsing check. Thanks for flagging - implementing now and finalizing my patch.
